MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jennifer Steneberg, appearing via Zoom as counsel for Petitioner, Rickey Allen Battaglia.

The court addressed the status of filing third account. Ms. Steneberg reported that the accounting is nearly complete, with two outstanding issues requiring clarification from the trustee. Due to the holidays, additional time was requested to finalize the filing. The court continues the status hearing to January 6, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Deputy: Singh; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Third Account.
Off Calendar Aug-28-2024 Continued To Nov-25-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Olga Krzyston appeared by zoom for Rickey Battaglia (petitioner).

Mrs. Krzyston requested a continuance stating that they are still waiting to get the account schedules from the petitioner and that she expects to get them next week. Status on filing of the third accounting is continued to November 25, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Nguyen. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Apr-05-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jennifer Steneberg, via ZOOM, counsel for Rickey Battaglia, Petitioner.

At the outset, the court queries Ms. Steneberg as to when the funds from the bank account were transferred to the investment side of CitiBank. Ms. Steneberg does not believe that has occurred and has not been able to confirm with her client as he is out of town. However, Ms. Steneberg believes that what was submitted to the court was a proposal. The court asks Ms. Steneberg whether she believes that if by following the proposal, if that would be in the best benefit of the Beneficiary and Ms. Steneberg agrees.

After further discussion, the matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Robert Woods; Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Feb-22-2021 Continued To Apr-05-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jennifer Steneberg appeared by zoom for Rickey Battaglia (Petitioner/Trustee).

The Court stated that the amount of money in the single account at Citi is $680,000.00, that it is over the $250,000.00 FDIC insured limit amount, expressed concern about the risk of potential loss to the special needs trust and stated that the funds need to be split into different financial institutions. The Court also asked Counsel to address the $1,200.00 disbursements for coffee, tea and gas. Ms. Steneberg stated that she believes the funds have now been invested and are diversified, and that she can file a declaration. Ms. Steneberg also addressed the $1,200.00 disbursement stating that this was a family vacation, that there was an agreement as to the cost of gas for driving back and forth, that the family took care of the costs for the hotel and the meals, that she has spoken with her Client and told him that this was not the right way to divide the shares and that going forward there needs to be a more accurate way to ensure a 1/3 share of the expenses to the trust. The Petition to Approve First Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is continued to April 5, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to provide a declaration regarding status of the bank account.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Bring Trust Under Probate Department Supervisor; For Authorization To Purchase Accessible Vehicle; To Pay Care Giving Fees; And To Approve
Off Calendar Mar-11-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jennifer Steneberg for and with Rickey Battaglia (Petitioner).
Christopher Calderon (Father of the Beneficiary) also present.

The Court stated that the substantive issues are the van and whether the proposal ought to be a little more concrete, rather than just providing the money for it and to what extend to allow compensation to the Parent for providing care. Ms. Steneberg stated that they have an estimate for the van with a purchase price of $54,610.86, that the Family is also working with the Regional Center for modifications for the wheelchair, that it will be a separate expense which she believes will be funded by the Regional Center and that the Beneficiary is significantly disabled. Ms. Steneberg also spoke of the Beneficiaries medical issues, her medical condition and her care needs, stated that both of the Parents provide 24/7 care, that the Parents take the Beneficiary to and from school, to therapy and to her medical appointments, and that the Parents provide a significant amount of care that is far more than what normal parenting would provide. In addition, Ms. Steneberg stated that she believes the request for caregiving is modest, that they are requesting a low hourly rate, that they are asking for eight hours a day, that there is an ex-parte petition to increase bond and that funding came in in May. The Petition to Bring Trust Under Probate Department Supervision, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.
